content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
Opinion Request Meeting Participants 
Date 
6-9-77 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Ted Prim 
Subject: 
Item for consideration. 
An independent polling of the staff indicates that we are 
split on the question of whether a lobbyist must report 
bills and expenses incidental to "volunteer" lobbying. 
If we cannot reach a solution at this opinion request 
meeting, perhaps we should consider taking this issue 
to the Commission. 
The three views are: 
TP: jo 
A. 
Once a lobbyist becomes a lobbyist all lobbying 
activities must be reported; 
B. 
A lobbyist must only report those activities 
performed as a result of his employment or con-
tractual relationship as a lobbyist; 
C. 
A lobbyist must report all lobbying activities 
except for those for which he is not compensated 
and which are totally unrelated to the interests 
of his employer. 
Thus,L~elobbyist for Standard 
Oil need not report his activities in connection 
with lobbying against smoking in public places 
but would have to report his activities in con-
nection with lobbying on mass transit legislation.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether lobbyists must report bills and expenses related to volunteer lobbying activities, and under what circumstances lobbying reporting obligations apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal FPPC memorandum, not a formal advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document presents three competing interpretations (A, B, C) of lobbying reporting requirements rather than FPPC's official position. No definitive FPPC response/conclusion provided; matter flagged for Commission consideration.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether lobbyists must report bills and expenses related to volunteer lobbying activities, and under what circumstances lobbying reporting obligations apply.

Once a lobbyist becomes a lobbyist all lobbying activities must be reported; B. A lobbyist must only report those activities performed as a result of his employment or con- tractual relationship as a lobbyist; C. A lobbyist must report all lobbying activities except for those for which he is not compensated and which are totally unrelated to the interests of his employer. embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum from Ted Prim to opinion request meeting participants dated June 9, 1977, presenting three conflicting staff views on whether and when lobbyists must report volunteer lobbying activities and related expenses.
